lead_signal.headline: No new FIMI operations identified this week — see coverage notes.
lead_signal.confidence_basis: Exhaustive search across all Tier 1–3 sources returned no new CIB reports, TAG bulletins, MSTIC/MTAC influence-operation disclosures, EEAS advisories, Stanford IO publications, DFRLab investigations, ASPI outputs, or Bellingcat FIMI investigations published during the week of 2026-05-08 to 2026-05-14. Most recent platform disclosures predate this window by at least 6–10 weeks.
lead_signal.note: Quiet-week determination is based on absence of new primary-source disclosures, not absence of activity. Structural gaps — particularly X/Twitter's lack of a CIB-equivalent disclosure mechanism and the shift by Meta to semiannual reporting cadence — mean that operational silence in the disclosure layer does not imply operational silence by threat actors. The most recent Meta Adversarial Threat Report (H1 2026, published 2026-03-11) and Google TAG Q4 2025 bulletin (published 2026-01-29) remain the current authoritative baselines.

campaigns.target: EU member states, Ukraine, US political audiences
campaigns.summary: Doppelganger is a persistent Russian influence operation first identified in 2022 that creates fake websites mimicking legitimate Western news outlets and amplifies pro-Russia, anti-Ukraine narratives. Stanford Internet Observatory confirmed that accounts linked to Doppelganger domains remained active on X/Twitter after Meta's takedowns, posing as Western news outlets and posting in English and French. Some network nodes migrated to Telegram and TikTok following Meta enforcement actions.
campaigns.new_this_week: No new enforcement actions or disclosures specific to Doppelganger were published this week. Baseline continuation assessed from prior reporting. No new infrastructure or domain additions confirmed in the current collection window.

sources_cited.excerpt: Accounts on X seemingly related to domains tied to Doppelganger continue to be active
We blocked 22 domains linked to Russia sharing content in multiple languages supportive of Russia

search_queries_run: Meta CIB coordinated inauthentic behaviour report May 2026
Google TAG threat analysis group influence operations May 2026
research_traceback.confidence_notes: Attribution to Russia is Confirmed based on multiple prior Tier 1 disclosures (Meta, Google TAG, EU EEAS). No new evidence this week — continuation assessed from established baseline. MF3 flagged because current-week independent verification of ongoing activity is not available from new primary sources.

campaigns.operation_name: Endless Mayfly / IUVM (IR) — Updated Attribution Baseline
campaigns.summary: Meta's H1 2026 Adversarial Threat Report (published 2026-03-11) provided updated attribution for the persistent Iranian influence operation known as Endless Mayfly, linking it to Iran's International Union of Virtual Media (IUVM). A separate Iranian operation began targeting English-speaking audiences in summer 2025 and was disrupted by Meta in Q4 2025 before it could build an authentic audience. Iran continues to rank as one of the three leading sources of foreign influence operations globally.
campaigns.new_this_week: No new disclosures this week. The March 2026 Meta report remains the current authoritative baseline for IUVM/Endless Mayfly attribution. No new enforcement actions or infrastructure additions confirmed in the current collection window.

sources_cited.source_name: Meta H1 2026 Integrity Reports / Adversarial Threat Report
sources_cited.excerpt: Updated attribution for persistent influence operation 'Endless Mayfly' attributed to Iran's IUVM
Russia, Iran and China continuing to serve as the three leading sources of foreign influence globally

search_queries_run: Meta CIB coordinated inauthentic behaviour report May 2026
Google TAG threat analysis group influence operations May 2026
research_traceback.confidence_notes: Attribution to IUVM/Iran is High based on Meta's own updated attribution in the March 2026 report. MF2 flagged because the IUVM attribution in the current cycle rests primarily on Meta's single-source disclosure without independent corroboration published this week.

campaigns.target: Western European audiences (EN, FR, DE, RU)
campaigns.summary: Google TAG's Q1 2025 bulletin (last updated May 15, 2025) documented the blocking of 57 domains linked to the Russian actor publicly tracked as Portal Kombat, sharing content in English, French, German, Russian and other languages supportive of Russia. This operation targets Western European information environments with pro-Russia narratives. No new TAG bulletin covering Q1 2026 or Q2 2026 has been published as of the current collection window.
campaigns.new_this_week: No new TAG bulletin published this week. The Q4 2025 bulletin (published 2026-01-29) remains the most recent Google TAG disclosure. A Q1 2026 bulletin is overdue based on historical cadence.

sources_cited.excerpt: Campaign linked to Portal Kombat sharing content in English, French, German, Russian

search_queries_run: Google TAG threat analysis group influence operations May 2026
research_traceback.confidence_notes: Attribution to Russia/Portal Kombat is High based on Google TAG's own designation. MF3 flagged because no new primary-source disclosure confirms current-week activity. Continuation assessed from established baseline.

actor_tracker.doctrine_note: Russia continues to operate multi-platform influence infrastructure combining fake news site spoofing (Doppelganger), IRA-linked YouTube channel networks, and AI-assisted content production. Operational doctrine emphasises narrative persistence across platform takedowns via migration to Telegram and TikTok. Coordination between content production units and RT/Sputnik amplification personnel remains a documented feature.
actor_tracker.headline: No new Russian FIMI disclosures this week; Doppelganger and Portal Kombat baselines persist from prior reporting.
actor_tracker.summary: Russia remains the most prolific documented source of foreign influence operations across Tier 1–3 sources, with active infrastructure confirmed across YouTube, Telegram, X/Twitter, and spoofed news domains. No new enforcement actions or attributions were published in the current collection window, but the absence of new disclosures reflects reporting cadence gaps rather than operational cessation.

actor_tracker.doctrine_note: China's primary documented influence infrastructure (Spamouflage/Taizi Flood) continues to operate at high volume, primarily via YouTube channel networks uploading spammy entertainment content with a small subset targeting US-China political narratives. Operational doctrine emphasises volume and platform saturation over precision targeting. No new China-specific FIMI disclosures were published this week.
actor_tracker.headline: No new Chinese FIMI disclosures this week; Spamouflage/Taizi Flood baseline persists from prior TAG and Meta reporting.
actor_tracker.summary: China ranks as one of the three leading sources of foreign influence operations per Meta's H1 2026 report. Google TAG's Q4 2025 bulletin documented ongoing large-scale YouTube channel terminations linked to PRC-affiliated operations. No new China-specific enforcement actions or attributions were published in the current collection window.

actor_tracker.doctrine_note: Iran's influence operations (IUVM/Endless Mayfly, Cotton Sandstorm) increasingly target English-speaking Western audiences with AI-assisted content. Meta's updated IUVM attribution and the disruption of a new English-language operation in Q4 2025 indicate continued investment in Western-facing influence infrastructure. IRGC-linked operations maintain a focus on Israel-Palestine narratives and US domestic political targeting.
actor_tracker.headline: Meta's H1 2026 report updated IUVM attribution for Endless Mayfly; new English-language operation disrupted in Q4 2025 before audience build-up.
actor_tracker.summary: Iran continues to operate persistent influence infrastructure with updated attribution to IUVM for Endless Mayfly. A new English-language operation targeting Western audiences was detected and disrupted by Meta in Q4 2025. No new Iran-specific disclosures were published in the current collection window.

actor_tracker.doctrine_note: Gulf state influence operations remain structurally underreported in Tier 1–3 sources. No new Gulf-attributed CIB disclosures, TAG bulletins, or OSINT investigations were published this week. The structural OSINT gap for Gulf actors reflects both lower platform disclosure rates and reduced academic/NGO research focus compared to Russia/China/Iran.
actor_tracker.headline: No Gulf-attributed FIMI disclosures identified this week; structural coverage gap persists.
actor_tracker.summary: Gulf state FIMI activity remains below the disclosure threshold for all monitored Tier 1–3 sources this week. This reflects a structural gap in platform transparency and OSINT coverage rather than confirmed absence of activity. Confidence in this assessment is low by design.

actor_tracker.doctrine_note: No US government-attributed domestic or foreign influence operations were disclosed by Tier 1–3 sources this week. The US Global Engagement Center (GEC) has not published new statements in the current collection window. Structural asymmetry in OSINT coverage means US-origin operations are systematically underreported relative to adversary actors.
actor_tracker.headline: No US-attributed FIMI disclosures identified this week; GEC has not published new statements.
actor_tracker.summary: No US-origin influence operations were disclosed or attributed by any monitored source this week. The GEC's operational posture and any ongoing counter-FIMI activities remain opaque in the current collection window. Absence of disclosure does not constitute absence of activity.

actor_tracker.doctrine_note: Google TAG's Q4 2025 bulletin documented the termination of 2 YouTube channels and 1 Ads account linked to Israel, sharing content in English critical of Canada. This represents the most recent confirmed Israeli-attributed influence operation disclosure. No new Israeli-attributed FIMI disclosures were published this week.
actor_tracker.headline: No new Israeli FIMI disclosures this week; Q4 2025 TAG bulletin remains most recent attribution.
actor_tracker.summary: The most recent confirmed Israeli-attributed influence operation disclosure is Google TAG's Q4 2025 bulletin, which documented a small operation targeting Canada. No new Israeli-attributed disclosures were published in the current collection window. Coverage of Israeli influence operations remains structurally thin across all monitored sources.

platform_responses.summary: Meta published its H1 2026 Adversarial Threat Report on March 11, 2026, covering influence operations from Russia, Iran, and China with updated IUVM attribution for Endless Mayfly and disclosure of a disrupted new Iranian English-language operation. This is the most recent Meta adversarial threat disclosure; no new report was published in the current collection window (week ending 2026-05-14). Meta has shifted to semiannual reporting cadence aligned with EU DSA transparency requirements.

Google TAG published its Q4 2025 influence operations bulletin on January 29, 2026, documenting terminations of YouTube channels linked to Russia (including IRA-linked channels targeting Moldova and Armenia), Israel (targeting Canada), Pakistan, Indonesia, and UK-based actors. No Q1 2026 TAG bulletin has been published as of the current collection window, representing a disclosure gap of approximately 15 weeks.

X/Twitter has no CIB-equivalent disclosure mechanism. No transparency report, influence operation takedown disclosure, or adversarial threat report was published by X/Twitter in the current collection window. Stanford Internet Observatory has documented that accounts linked to Russian Doppelganger domains and other CIB networks identified by Meta remain active on X/Twitter without corresponding X enforcement actions. This absence of disclosure is recorded separately from absence of activity per schema requirements.

Microsoft's most recent threat intelligence publication in the current monitoring period attributed a supply chain attack on the Axios npm package to North Korean state actor Sapphire Sleet (published March 31, 2026). No new MSTIC/MTAC influence-operation-specific report was published in the current collection window. Microsoft's most recent FIMI-relevant reporting predates this week.

cognitive_warfare.headline: AI-assisted tradecraft escalation confirmed across Russia, Iran, China in Meta H1 2026 baseline
cognitive_warfare.detail: Meta's H1 2026 Adversarial Threat Report (published 2026-03-11) confirmed that increasing sophistication and AI-assisted tradecraft was a cross-cutting theme in disrupted covert influence operations from Russia, Iran, and China. Meta also documented the use of AI by adversarial actors to violate Community Standards more broadly, alongside Meta's own deployment of advanced AI systems for content enforcement. The convergence of offensive AI use by threat actors and defensive AI deployment by platforms represents a structural shift in the cognitive warfare environment.
cognitive_warfare.significance: The simultaneous escalation of AI-assisted offensive tradecraft and AI-driven defensive enforcement creates an arms-race dynamic that is likely to compress detection windows and increase the operational tempo of influence campaigns. The cognitive warfare implication is that AI-generated content at scale may increasingly saturate platform moderation capacity, particularly on platforms without CIB-equivalent disclosure mechanisms (X/Twitter, Telegram).

cognitive_warfare.headline: Platform migration pattern: CIB networks shift to Telegram and TikTok following Meta enforcement
cognitive_warfare.detail: Stanford Internet Observatory documented that accounts linked to Russian Doppelganger networks disrupted by Meta migrated activity to Telegram and TikTok, continuing to post in English about Ukraine, Russia, and US politics. Seven Doppelganger-linked domains maintained active X/Twitter accounts posing as Western news outlets. This migration pattern — from enforced platforms to less-regulated alternatives — is a documented and recurring feature of Russian influence infrastructure.
cognitive_warfare.significance: Platform migration following enforcement actions demonstrates the resilience of well-resourced influence operations and the structural limitation of single-platform takedowns. The shift to Telegram (which has minimal content moderation) and TikTok (which has limited CIB disclosure transparency) represents a cognitive warfare adaptation that reduces the effectiveness of coordinated platform enforcement.

attribution_log.headline: Meta updates attribution for Endless Mayfly to Iran's International Union of Virtual Media (IUVM)
attribution_log.summary: Meta's H1 2026 Adversarial Threat Report provided updated attribution for the persistent influence operation known as Endless Mayfly, linking it to Iran's International Union of Virtual Media (IUVM). This represents a refinement of prior attribution and is the most recent formal attribution update for this operation. No independent corroboration from a second Tier 1 source was identified in the current collection window.

attribution_log.headline: Google TAG Q4 2025 bulletin attributes 2 YouTube channels and 1 Ads account to Israel, targeting Canada
attribution_log.summary: Google TAG's Q4 2025 bulletin documented the termination of 2 YouTube channels and 1 Ads account linked to Israel, sharing content in English critical of Canada. This is a low-volume operation with limited documented impact. Attribution is assessed rather than confirmed due to the absence of corroborating Tier 1 or Tier 2 sources. The operation's targeting of Canada is analytically notable given the broader context of Israeli information operations related to the Gaza conflict.

attribution_log.headline: Google TAG Q4 2025 bulletin confirms IRA-linked YouTube channel terminations targeting Moldova and Armenia
attribution_log.summary: Google TAG's Q4 2025 bulletin documented the termination of multiple YouTube channel clusters linked to Russia's Internet Research Agency (IRA), targeting Moldova (content in Romanian and Russian) and Armenia (content in Russian and Armenian). A separate cluster shared content in German supportive of a German political party and critical of the EU. Attribution to the IRA is confirmed based on Google TAG's own designation, consistent with prior reporting.

cross_monitor_signals.wdm: No new FIMI operations targeting democratic processes in WDM-monitored countries were disclosed this week. The most recent relevant baseline is Meta's H1 2026 report confirming ongoing Russian operations targeting Moldova and EU member states, and Google TAG Q4 2025 documenting IRA-linked operations targeting Moldovan political narratives. No election-cycle-specific FIMI disclosures were published in the current window.
cross_monitor_signals.scem: No new FIMI operations explicitly linked to active conflict escalation events were disclosed this week. The ongoing Russia-Ukraine information environment remains the primary conflict-adjacent FIMI theatre based on prior reporting. No new conflict-linked cognitive warfare disclosures were published in the current collection window.
cross_monitor_signals.esa: Meta's H1 2026 baseline confirms ongoing Russian operations targeting EU member states and EU-critical narratives (Moldova, Germany, broader Western European audiences via Portal Kombat). Google TAG Q4 2025 documented a Russian operation sharing content critical of the EU in German. No new EU-institution-specific FIMI disclosures were published this week. EU DSA enforcement actions against platforms for FIMI-related transparency failures were not identified in the current collection window.
cross_monitor_signals.agm: Meta's H1 2026 report confirmed AI-assisted tradecraft escalation across Russia, Iran, and China, with adversarial actors using AI to violate Community Standards and Meta deploying advanced AI systems for defensive enforcement. Microsoft's threat intelligence blog documented AI operationalisation by nation-state actors for disinformation at scale. No new AI-specific FIMI capability disclosures were published in the current collection window beyond these established baselines.

weekly_brief_narrative:
Week ending 2026-05-14 is assessed as a quiet week for new FIMI disclosures across all monitored Tier 1–3 sources. No new CIB reports, platform adversarial threat disclosures, government attribution statements, or Tier 2–3 OSINT investigations were published during the collection window of 2026-05-08 to 2026-05-14.

The current authoritative baselines are: Meta's H1 2026 Adversarial Threat Report (published 2026-03-11), which confirmed Russia, Iran, and China as the three leading sources of foreign influence operations and updated attribution for Iran's Endless Mayfly operation to IUVM; and Google TAG's Q4 2025 bulletin (published 2026-01-29), which documented IRA-linked YouTube channel terminations targeting Moldova and Armenia, a Russian operation targeting Germany with EU-critical content, and a small Israeli-attributed operation targeting Canada.

Three structural observations are analytically significant this week. First, Meta's shift to semiannual reporting cadence (aligned with EU DSA requirements) means the next adversarial threat report is not expected until approximately September 2026, creating a six-month disclosure gap in the most comprehensive Tier 1 source. Second, Google TAG's Q1 2026 bulletin is overdue based on historical quarterly cadence, representing a disclosure gap of approximately 15 weeks. Third, X/Twitter continues to operate without any CIB-equivalent disclosure mechanism; Stanford Internet Observatory has documented that Doppelganger-linked accounts remain active on X/Twitter without corresponding enforcement actions, and this structural gap is recorded as an absence of disclosure rather than an absence of activity.

Actor posture assessments remain unchanged from prior weeks. Russia is assessed as highly active across multi-platform infrastructure with documented migration of disrupted networks to Telegram and TikTok. China's Spamouflage/Taizi Flood infrastructure continues at high volume per prior TAG reporting. Iran's IUVM-linked operations are active with confirmed English-language Western-audience targeting. Gulf, US, and Israeli actor coverage remains structurally thin, reflecting both lower platform disclosure rates and reduced Tier 2–3 research focus.

The most analytically significant development in the current baseline period — though not new this week — is the confirmed convergence of AI-assisted offensive tradecraft by Russia, Iran, and China with AI-driven defensive enforcement by Meta. This arms-race dynamic is likely to compress detection windows and increase operational tempo in the next reporting cycle. Analysts should monitor for the overdue Google TAG Q1 2026 bulletin and any mid-cycle Meta disclosures as the primary near-term signal sources.

research_coverage.x_twitter_gap_note: X/Twitter has no CIB-equivalent disclosure mechanism and published no transparency report, influence operation takedown notice, or adversarial threat disclosure in the current collection window. Stanford Internet Observatory has documented that accounts linked to Russian Doppelganger networks and other CIB operations identified by Meta remain active on X/Twitter without corresponding X enforcement actions. The absence of X/Twitter disclosure is recorded as a structural transparency gap, not as evidence of absence of activity. This gap is persistent and structural, not specific to this week.

open_findings.headline: Meta shifts to semiannual adversarial threat reporting cadence aligned with EU DSA, creating a six-month disclosure gap between H1 2026 report (March 2026) and next expected report (approximately September 2026).
open_findings.why_it_matters: The shift from quarterly to semiannual reporting reduces the temporal resolution of the most comprehensive Tier 1 FIMI disclosure source, creating extended windows during which new influence operations may operate without public platform-level attribution — a structural degradation of the FIMI monitoring ecosystem that benefits well-resourced persistent actors.

open_findings.headline: Google TAG Q1 2026 influence operations bulletin is overdue by approximately 15 weeks based on historical quarterly cadence, with no publication date announced.
open_findings.why_it_matters: The absence of a Q1 2026 TAG bulletin means that influence operation enforcement actions taken by Google between January and May 2026 have not been publicly disclosed, representing a significant gap in the Tier 1 disclosure record and reducing the ability of researchers and governments to track emerging infrastructure.

open_findings.headline: Meta's H1 2026 report documents removal of over 10.9 million accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia and the Middle East in 2025, with growing sophistication and industrialisation of these operations.
open_findings.why_it_matters: The industrialisation of scam center infrastructure — which overlaps with influence operation tradecraft in its use of fake accounts, coordinated inauthentic behaviour, and cross-platform amplification — represents an emerging convergence between financially motivated fraud networks and state-adjacent information manipulation ecosystems that the standard FIMI analytical framework may underweight.

open_findings.headline: Microsoft Threat Intelligence confirmed North Korean state actor Sapphire Sleet conducted a supply chain attack on the Axios npm package on March 31, 2026, demonstrating continued DPRK capability to weaponise developer infrastructure.
open_findings.why_it_matters: While primarily a cyber operation rather than a FIMI operation, the Sapphire Sleet supply chain attack illustrates the convergence of cyber intrusion and influence operation infrastructure — a pattern documented in prior DPRK, Russian, and Iranian operations — and signals that DPRK should be monitored as an emerging FIMI actor in addition to its established cyber role.
